Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263) for MITRE tagging export.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=grc` `sourcetype="isac:draft_quality"` (draft_id, technique_count, ioc_count, pii_scrub_score, reviewer).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Never store raw customer narratives—use scores only; (2) train reviewers quarterly; (3) integrate STIX export validator; (4) track improvement trend; (5) align with NCSC/FINCERT local guidance.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=grc sourcetype="isac:draft_quality" earliest=-180d
| where technique_count<3 OR ioc_count<5 OR pii_scrub_score<0.95
| stats count by reviewer
| sort - count
```

Understanding this SPL

**DORA Information Sharing — Anonymized Incident TTP Contribution Quality Metrics** — Quality of shared technical details (MITRE technique coverage, IOC richness) determines community usefulness. Internal scoring of contributions before external send improves consistency and evidences a mature sharing culture without leaking sensitive data in Splunk.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=grc` `sourcetype="isac:draft_quality"` (draft_id, technique_count, ioc_count, pii_scrub_score, reviewer). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263) for MITRE tagging export. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: grc; **sourcetype**: isac:draft_quality. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=grc, sourcetype="isac:draft_quality", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where technique_count<3 OR ioc_count<5 OR pii_scrub_score<0.95` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by reviewer** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Scatter (technique vs ioc), Table (low-quality drafts), Single value (reject rate).
grandmaExplanation: We watch for quality of shared technical details (mitre technique coverage, ioc richness) determines community usefulness. internal scoring of contributions before external send improves consistency and evidenc. This helps maintain steady compliance posture and reduces manual review effort.
